theme: hash function versus fifty hostile key patterns
title: Hash Function Versus the Published Key-Family Sweep
strategies:
mod-M reduction is a bijective relabeling of x mod M, so any pipeline that reduces
     via MODM without an avalanche stage first is defeated by an AP family whose stride
     is a multiple of M, regardless of the multiplier/offset chosen

a single fixed textbook multiplicative constant (Fibonacci hashing) has an exact
     algebraic resonance: pick an AP stride S with A0*S = small_delta (mod 2^64), and the
     whole family collapses into a handful of adjacent top-bit buckets

reading the actual published sweep and searching per-instance for a multiplier that
     avoids every planted resonance (instead of trusting one canonical constant) recovers
     near-pigeonhole-optimal worst-case load on every family type in the sweep
